fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

reasons: Validation-style hints require release-country in country name format, so country canonicalization should follow name output style.

Track fields are explicitly treated as list-like in the validation/context directives, and diagnostics mention malformed_list_encoding for tracks_track_name plus recommendations for canonical list serialization.
Reasoning states fusion logic is incompatible with list-normalized inputs for genre/label and that track list outputs need consistent canonical formatting and ordering.
There is no evidence of case-only mismatches or country near-mismatches, so broad lowercasing or aggressive text normalization is not supported.
Most failures are coverage/fusion related, so normalization should be rerun only as a targeted secondary fix.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun targeted normalization for release-country using country names and for the track list fields using consistent canonical list serialization. Also ensure genre and label handling is compatible with normalized list/scalar inputs, but do not apply broad lowercasing or other text-style changes because validation style preserves original casing and diagnostics show no case-only mismatch signal. Normalization is secondary to the larger coverage and fusion-policy issues.

investigator_action_plan.evidence_summary: Cluster analysis flagged unhealthy pair(s): correspondences_musicbrainz_lastfm.csv.
Fused rows (32848) are 88.17% of total source rows (37255), suggesting low consolidation.
Validation accuracy is especially weak for selected attributes and indicates fusion policy misalignment rather than only normalization noise.
High fused-to-source row ratio implies many records survived as separate entities; review whether true matches are being missed.
lastfm dominates agreement for duration (similarity 1.0 vs musicbrainz 0.763439, support 3327).

findings.title: Fused output size is close to total source size, indicating limited cross-source consolidation.
Several attributes have poor validation accuracy.
One or more pairwise correspondence files indicate over-clustering / many-to-many matches.

evidence.overall_diagnosis: One or more correspondence files show a non-long-tail distribution.

parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

findings.title: Missing fused values remain frequent in evaluation debug traces.
Fusion size estimates materially underpredict actual fused size.

recommendations.evidence_summary: Cluster analysis flagged unhealthy pair(s): correspondences_musicbrainz_lastfm.csv.

cluster_parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

recommendations.evidence_summary: Fused rows (32848) are 88.17% of total source rows (37255), suggesting low consolidation.
Validation accuracy is especially weak for selected attributes and indicates fusion policy misalignment rather than only normalization noise.
High fused-to-source row ratio implies many records survived as separate entities; review whether true matches are being missed.
Size estimator underpredicts actual fused rows by roughly 16-19%, which weakens planning and makes matching/fusion drift harder to interpret.
Low-accuracy attributes overlap normalization-sensitive columns; add schema and serialization validation after normalization and before fusion.

PyDI.fusion.evaluation module

Evaluation framework for data fusion in PyDI.
This module provides tools for evaluating the quality of fusion results
against validation/test datasets.

(
name
=

[source]

Bases:
object
Strategy for data fusion that manages attribute fusers and evaluation rules.
A strategy defines how to fuse each attribute by registering AttributeValueFuser
instances and optional evaluation rules for quality assessment.
Parameters
:
name
(
str
)
add_attribute_fuser
(
attribute
,
fuser_or_resolver
,
evaluation_function
=
None
,
**
fuser_kwargs
)
[source]

Register how to fuse an attribute (unified API).
Accepts either a pre-built AttributeValueFuser (advanced usage) or a
resolver function (simple usage). When a resolver function is provided,
it is wrapped into an AttributeValueFuser with any extra keyword args
supplied via
fuser_kwargs
(e.g., accessor=…, required=True).
Parameters
:

-----
